body:
## 🚨 Solution Draft Failed
The automated solution draft encountered an error:
```
The solver stopped before creating a pull request.

Reason: Repository setup halted - existing fork replacement could lose commits.

What happened:
- Expected fork or replacement repository: konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra
- Expected upstream: Payel-git-ol/Octra
- Actual state: konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra is not a GitHub fork of Payel-git-ol/Octra.
- Safety check: GitHub compare returned 404 Not Found, so Hive Mind could not prove the repository has no unique commits.

Why it stopped:
Hive Mind did not delete konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra because the repository may contain commits that would be lost.

Options:
1. Back up any needed work in konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra, then delete, rename, archive, or repair that repository in GitHub and rerun the solver.
2. If you do not control konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra, ask the repository owner or a Hive Mind administrator to clean it up and rerun the solver.
3. Rerun with --allow-force-non-fork-repository-deletion only after confirming that deleting konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra is acceptable.
```

### What happened
- Hive Mind found `konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra` where it needed a GitHub fork of `Payel-git-ol/Octra`.
- It could not prove whether the existing repository has unique commits, so it did not delete it automatically.

### What you can do
- Repository owner path: back up any needed work, then delete, rename, archive, or repair `konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra` in GitHub and rerun the solver.
- External-owner path: ask the repository owner or a Hive Mind administrator to clean it up and rerun the solver.
- Use `--allow-force-non-fork-repository-deletion` only after confirming that deleting `konard/Payel-git-ol-Octra` is acceptable and any unique commits can be lost.

### 🤖 **Models used:**
- Tool: OpenAI Codex
- Requested: `gpt-5.5`
- Thinking level: max (~31999 tokens)
- **Model: GPT-5.5** (`gpt-5.5`)

<details>
